title: OptiMind User Profile
description: Canonical schema for user_profile.json. The runtime MUST validate the loaded profile against this schema and refuse to start on incompatibility. PENDING semantics: any rule with confidence < 0.5 is treated as machine-readable PENDING (not just human convention).

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump via a migrations/user_profile_<from>to<to>.py script.

topic.description: Enforced enum so a typo (e.g. 'nuturition') fails validation rather than silently creating an orphan rule the Analyst agent will never grep. Reflects the real categories in use (supplementation is the largest); add a new topic here before using it.

source.description: Free-text provenance — where this rule came from. Conventions for new rules: 'user_interaction', 'agent_learned' (reflection-promoted; the decay job only fades these), 'manual'. Legacy/curated rules may carry a descriptive source (e.g. 'Supplement Protocol Review (2026-05-05)'); these are intentionally exempt from decay. Not an enum — provenance is open-ended.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime with explicit offset. Bare 'Z' (UTC) suffix is banned — every timestamp must carry the user's offset (US/Eastern by default) per the timezone binding in CLAUDE.md.

confidence.description: Below 0.5 is the PENDING threshold: the rule is machine-readable but not yet authoritative. Agents should surface PENDING rules for confirmation rather than apply them silently.
